title: Bug: path-only /documents endpoint reads relative to process CWD instead of repo root
description:
## Summary

`JsonFileStorage::read_path_bytes` (crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:745-754, working-tree branch) calls `fs::read(path)` directly. Path is repo-root-relative (server/UI/search all pass repo-root-relative paths), but `fs::read` resolves against the process's current working directory.

Result: when the jit server is started from anywhere other than the repo root, `/documents?path=foo/bar.md` and `/documents/raw?path=foo/bar.md` fail to find files that exist in the repo.

The git-commit branch of the same function (lines 712-744) correctly resolves against `self.root.parent()` (the repo root, parent of `.jit/`). The working-tree branch should use the same root.

## Reproduction

```bash
cd /tmp && /path/to/target/release/jit serve --root /home/user/some-repo
# In another terminal:
curl http://localhost:3000/api/documents?path=README.md
# 404 — but README.md exists in /home/user/some-repo
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `JsonFileStorage::read_path_bytes` working-tree branch resolves `path` against the repo root (parent of `self.root`), matching the behavior of the git-commit branch — covered by a unit test that opens a JsonFileStorage with a non-CWD root and confirms the read succeeds.
- [ ] The `/documents?path=...` success-path test in `crates/server/src/routes.rs` (currently a stub at lines 1120-1145 that the agent self-acknowledged was not meaningfully testing the success case) is replaced with a real assertion: writes a fixture file at a path inside the repo root, hits the endpoint, and asserts both the response status and the response body bytes.
- [ ] `cargo-ci` gate passes.
- [ ] `code-review` gate passes.

## Related

Story abfd6016 (multi-format doc rendering). Surfaced during task 42bd50ce code-review re-run after task 75b1ccb8 was marked done. The finding actually belongs to task 75b1ccb8's storage refactor; tracked as a separate bug rather than re-opening 75b1ccb8.
